title: Here’s the Celtics’ reported asking price for Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics are reportedly seeking at least four first-round picks for Jaylen Brown in trade negotiations.
Jaylen Brown served as the primary piece in Boston's unsuccessful trade bid for Giannis Antetokounmpo.
Celtics President Brad Stevens did not dismiss the possibility of trading Brown while discussing the team's future.

title: Norway vs. France live updates: ‘Boston Stadium’ set to host high-profile World Cup matchup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Norway vs. France World Cup match takes place at Gillette Stadium, called 'Boston Stadium', in Foxborough.
Norwegian striker Erling Haaland starts on the bench as Norway rests several starters despite clinching knockout stage.
France's coach Didier Deschamps misses match due to his mother's funeral; Assistant Guy Stéphan steps in.

title: When Pickles the dog saved the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: In 1966, Pickles the dog found the stolen Jules Rimet Trophy in South London, making him a national hero.
The trophy's theft occurred shortly before England's first World Cup hosting, causing a significant national scandal.
Pickles was honored at England's World Cup celebrations after they won their only title, defeating West Germany 4-2.

title: More than 4,000 Mass General Brigham nurses to strike on July 8
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Approximately 4,500 nurses and clinicians at Mass General Brigham will strike on July 8 for better labor terms.
The strike includes Brigham and Women’s Hospital and Mass General Brigham Home Care due to stalled contract negotiations.
Mass General Brigham claims its nurses receive competitive compensation and remains committed to reaching a fair agreement.

title: Wellesley man pleads not guilty to stabbing his father to death at playground
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: James Floyd Bennett III pleads not guilty to first-degree murder in father's stabbing at Wellesley playground.
Victim James Floyd Bennett Jr. suffered about 20 stab wounds; incident occurred after discussing World Cup.
Suspect turned himself in after alleged murder; family supports him despite his mental health history.

title: Young boy hospitalized after falling from second-floor window in Worcester
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 4-year-old boy fell from a second-story window in Worcester on Thursday, police reported.
The child was transported to a local hospital and is in stable condition with bumps and bruises.
Worcester police are investigating the incident, which occurred on Southold Road around 5 p.m.

title: 12-year-old dead, another child injured after bus collides with motorized scooter in Haverhill
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 12-year-old girl died and another child was injured after a bus hit their scooter in Haverhill.
Police found the injured children at Portland Street and 5th Avenue; one later died at a hospital.
Haverhill schools Superintendent Marotta urged respect for the family’s privacy and discussed scooter safety.

title: 4 takeaways from the U.S. loss against Türkiye in the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The U.S. lost 3-2 to Türkiye in their final group stage match at the 2026 World Cup.
Despite the loss, the U.S. topped Group D and will face Bosnia and Herzegovina in the knockout stage.
Matt Turner started in goal as the U.S. opted to rest regular starters after securing a knockout round spot.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com releases a news quiz covering topics like Mike Wankum's departure and Tall Ships visits.
The quiz includes questions related to the World Cup and other trending stories in Greater Boston.
Peter Chianca, general assignment editor since 2019, authored the June 26, 2026, Greater Boston news quiz.

title: Moulton allegedly knocks phone from questioner’s hand after being asked about Graham Platner
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rep. Seth Moulton allegedly knocked a phone from a person's hand when asked about endorsing Graham Platner.
Moulton's campaign claims the person recording was a 'paid political tracker' from America Rising, not a reporter.
Fox News initially identified the individual as a reporter but later acknowledged them as a tracker after a campaign statement.
